purpose: Meta-model combining key signals from all 3 models + their tier scores to produce a holistic qualification score.

features.reason: Tier output of Consumer Score Model — High/Medium/Low consumer readiness
Tier output of Interaction Score Model — High/Medium/Low engagement quality
Tier output of Product Quotation Score Model — High/Medium/Low deal seriousness
Buying intent label — one of the strongest single predictors carried forward from consumer model
Milestone presence signals active deal progression
Speed through pipeline — fast movers qualify and close faster
Historical win track record — persistent signal across all domains
Repeat customers have a lower qualification bar across all scoring dimensions
Industry baseline — calibrates the score against sector-level conversion difficulty
Historical sentiment trajectory — positive trend boosts qualification confidence
Volume of engagement — highly interacted leads are better qualified
Multi-channel engagement = stronger buyer intent
Recency of last contact — stale leads should be flagged as low qualification
Quality depth of engagement beyond raw count
How deep into follow-up cycle the lead went — reflects persistence of both sides
Whether lead responded to follow-ups at all — direct re-engagement signal
Normalized follow-up response rate — avoids bias from high-interaction leads
Agent consistency in engagement — poor response increases qualification risk
Quotation sent is the strongest pre-close structural signal
Enterprise vs SMB — different qualification criteria and scoring thresholds apply
Current funnel stage is essential for holistic qualification scoring
Time in pipeline reflects deal health — too long signals a stuck or at-risk deal
Speed of overall progression — fast movers at qualification stage are close to closing
Deal value — larger deals need more signals to qualify; low-value deals close faster
Chiller vs Cooler affects the typical qualification journey and cycle length
Bucketed capacity captures deal size category — large-capacity deals have different qualification dynamics
Free installation reduces buyer friction — affects the qualification conversion threshold
Revision count reflects negotiation depth — higher count = engaged but uncertain buyer
Delivery channel of quotation — affects review rate and response speed
Incomplete leads are structurally harder to qualify — missing data limits scoring confidence
Lead origin affects qualification quality — referral leads qualify at higher rates than cold inbound
